summary: Executed backend pytest suite (7 tests) + Playwright end-to-end verification of Vennela portfolio elevation. 6/7 backend and 15/15 frontend acceptance criteria pass. Only failure: slowapi rate limit is not being enforced on /api/contact (sent 7 requests → all 200, no 429). Everything else — health, contact success (Resend returns id), 422 validation for invalid email / empty message / empty name, honeypot silent-ok, gate overlay + skip, hero eyebrow 'Product · Research · Design', headline, all sections, magic mirror personalisation, inline field errors, butterfly delivery overlay + confirmation card text, ESC/close dismissal, Konami code secret note, long-press VR bloom, mobile 400×800 layout, and zero console/page errors — all verified working.

minor.issue: Rate limit (@limiter.limit('5/minute')) not enforced. 7 rapid requests all returned 200 instead of 429 on the 6th+. Likely cause: behind Kubernetes ingress, get_remote_address returns the same internal proxy IP AND/OR slowapi middleware not registered — but even with same IP the limit should trigger, so probable root cause is that slowapi's SlowAPIMiddleware is not added (only exception handler + app.state.limiter are set). Fix: add `app.add_middleware(SlowAPIMiddleware)` from slowapi.middleware, and use a key_func that reads X-Forwarded-For (get_ipaddr) to identify real client IPs behind the ingress.

issues: #bfConfirm computed opacity was 0 briefly after the overlay reached 'active' state — the card fades in via CSS transition. Not a bug, just noting that automated timing checks need ~5-6s wait for full opacity. Text content is correct: 'The butterflies have done their job.' + 'I'll take it from here.'

action_items: Wire slowapi middleware so the 5/minute limit actually enforces. Recommended: `from slowapi.middleware import SlowAPIMiddleware; app.add_middleware(SlowAPIMiddleware)` and use `get_ipaddr` (which honors X-Forwarded-For) as key_func so different real clients get distinct buckets behind the ingress.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py loads env keys via `os.environ[...]` at import time — fine for fail-fast but crashes uvicorn if any of RESEND_API_KEY/SENDER_EMAIL/CONTACT_RECIPIENT is missing. Consider a clearer startup error message.
CORS is wide open (allow_origins=['*']) with allow_credentials=True — this combination is ignored by browsers but should be tightened to the production origin for hygiene.
index.html is a single ~1185 line file — acceptable for a static portfolio but candidates for extraction: contact form, butterfly overlay, secret note into partials if the project grows.
script.js contains multiple IIFE blocks and duplicate DOM lookups; not a bug but a maintainability note.
Contact form allows autocomplete on the honeypot field would defeat the trap — good that autocomplete='off' is set on #cfCompany.
No CSRF token on POST /api/contact — acceptable given anonymous public form + honeypot + rate limit, but the rate limit currently doesn't fire (see backend issue).
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this project
seed_data_creation: None. Test contact submissions used TEST_ prefixed name and a test+butterfly@example.com email; the real Resend account received them from onboarding@resend.dev → vennela.rudraraju@gmail.com.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend runs at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Contact API accepts {name,email,message,company(honeypot)}. Frontend is plain HTML/CSS/JS served by Vite (no React). Gate overlay always appears — click #gateSkipBtn to bypass. Konami code uses ArrowUp ArrowUp ArrowDown ArrowDown ArrowLeft ArrowRight ArrowLeft ArrowRight b a (KeyB/KeyA also work). Magic mirror uses a typing animation — wait 5s after clicking Ask before asserting text. Butterfly confirmation card takes ~5s to fade in fully after form submit (overlay active class fires immediately). Backend rate limit currently not enforced — bug open.
rca of the issue: Rate limit not firing: FastAPI + slowapi requires either SlowAPIMiddleware or SlowAPIASGIMiddleware to actually intercept requests and check the limiter buckets. The current server.py only sets app.state.limiter and registers the exception handler, which means the @limiter.limit decorator on the route is effectively a no-op in this configuration. Additionally, behind Kubernetes ingress, request.client.host returns the ingress IP, so if the middleware were added, every request would share one bucket — swap key_func to slowapi.util.get_ipaddr (which parses X-Forwarded-For) to attribute correctly to real clients. Reproduction: run test_rate_limit_5_per_minute in /app/backend/tests/test_contact_api.py — all 7 responses return 200.
